methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
SpectralX is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by IDF special-forces veterans. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public firms operating in the Defense & Security sector.

The peer-median method anchors the analysis. Three same-stage, same-sector comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yield a median valuation of $300M, with an observed range of $150M to $500M. This represents the primary triangulation input given available data and methodology applicability. The peer set also includes established private competitors such as Anduril Industries and Scale AI, alongside public benchmarks (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), though quantitative inputs from these were not applied to the core calculation.

The triangulated valuation range is $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single applicable method (peer-median) across a limited sample of three direct comparables. Key uncertainties include SpectralX's undisclosed capital raise to date, the absence of revenue or profitability metrics in the dataset, and the discrete nature of the peer sample. Wider dispersion in the comparable set ($150M–$500M) signals material variation in how Defense & Security Series A firms are valued, suggesting execution quality and product differentiation remain critical valuation drivers not captured by median benchmarking alone.
